-- We need to show that Φ₂ r has a finite integral over [0, 1].
  -- Since Φ₂ is continuous on [0, 1], it is bounded on this interval.
  -- We can use the fact that continuous functions on compact intervals are bounded and hence have finite integrals.
  have h₁ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (Set.Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₂ : IsCompact (Set.Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ) := isCompact_Icc
  -- Use the fact that the integral of a continuous function on a compact interval is finite.
  have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the property of continuous functions on compact sets to show the integral is finite.
    apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
    <;> aesop
  -- Since Φ₂ r is continuous and [0, 1] is compact, the integral is finite.
  exact h₃

            simp [Φ₂]
            <;>

            (try
              linarith) <;>
            (try
              simp_all [hr]) <;>
            (try
              nlinarith)
          linarith
        exact h₇
      exact Set.mem_Iic.mpr h₆
    -- Use the boundedness to show that the integral is finite
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by

    -- Use the fact that Φ₂ r is continuous on [0, 1] and the interval is compact to deduce integrability

    have h₅ : MeasurableSet (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := measurableSet_Icc
    have h₆ : (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)).toFinite := by
      -- The interval [0, 1] has finite volume
      refine' Set.Finite.toFinite _
      apply Set.finite_Icc
    -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is integrable
    have h₇ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;>

            -- Prove that the set is compact
            apply isCompact_Icc
            , by
            -- Prove that the set is measurable
            exact measurableSet_Icc⟩
        })
    exact h₇
  exact h₃

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc 0 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₄ : (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1).volume < ∞ := by
      simp [Real.volume_Icc]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on a compact set to deduce it is bounded
    -- and thus has finite integral
    have h₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) :=
      (hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuousOn_of_isCompact (by continuity) h₃)
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

  refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_hasFiniteIntegral_restrict_of_null (by
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on [0, 1] and the interval has finite measure.
    have h : ∃ (c : ℝ), ∀ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |Φ₂ r x| ≤ c := by
      -- Since Φ₂ is continuous on [0, 1], it is bounded.
      have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₂ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := Metric.isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded.mp h₂ |>.2
      obtain ⟨d, hd⟩ := Metric.isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded.mp (h₂.compact_image h₁) |>.2
      refine' ⟨d, _⟩
      intro x hx
      have h₃ : (Φ₂ r x : ℝ) ∈ Set.Icc (-d : ℝ) d := by
        have h₄ : (Φ₂ r x : ℝ) ∈ Set.Icc (-d : ℝ) d := by
          have h₅ : (Φ₂ r x : ℝ) ∈ (h₁.image (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)).closure := by
            apply subset_closure
            apply h₁.mem_image_of_mem
            exact hx
          have h₆ : (h₁.image (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)).closure ⊆ Set.Icc (-d : ℝ) d :=
            by
              have h₇ : (h₁.image (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)).closure ⊆ Set.Icc (-d : ℝ) d :=
                Metric.closure_minimal (by
                  -- Show that h₁.image (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) is bounded by d
                  have h₈ : ∀ y ∈ (h₁.image (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)), |y| ≤ d := by
                    intro y hy
                    obtain ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩ := h₁.mem_image.mp hy
                    have h₉ : |Φ₂ r x| < d := by
                      have h₁₀ : (x : ℝ) ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hx
                      have h₁₁ : |(Φ₂ r x : ℝ)| < d := by
                        have h₁₂ := hd x hx
                        simp_all [Real.dist_eq]
                        <;> linarith
                      exact h₁₁
                    have h₁₀ : |y| < d := by
                      simpa [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos] using h₉
                    linarith
                  -- Use the fact that the set is bounded to show it is contained in Icc (-d) d
                  intro y hy
                  have h₁₁ : |y| ≤ d := by
                    obtain ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩ := h₁.mem_image.mp hy
                    have h₁₂ := hd x hx
                    simp_all [Real.dist_eq]
                    <;> linarith
                  have h₁₃ : -d ≤ y := by
                    linarith [abs_le.mp h₁₁]
                  have h₁₄ : y ≤ d := by
                    linarith [abs_le.mp h₁₁]
                  exact ⟨h₁₃, h₁₄⟩
                ) (by
                  -- Show that Icc (-d) d is closed
                  exact isClosed_Icc
                )
              exact h₇
            exact h₆
          have h₈ : (Φ₂ r x : ℝ) ∈ Set.Icc (-d : ℝ) d := by
            apply h₆
            apply h₅
          exact h₈
        exact h₄
      have h₉ : |(Φ₂ r x : ℝ)| ≤ d := by
        simp only [abs_le] at h₃ ⊢
        constructor <;> linarith
      exact h₉
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := h
    -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to show that the integral is finite.
    have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (c : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- The constant function has a finite integral over [0, 1].
      have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (c : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
        convert hasFiniteIntegral_const.volume_restrict (Set.Icc 0 1) using 1
        <;> simp [volume_Icc]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> linarith
      exact h₂
    -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to show that the integral is finite.
    have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => |Φ₂ r x|) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that |Φ₂ r x| ≤ c to show that the integral is finite.
      have h₃ : ∀ x ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ), |Φ₂ r x| ≤ (c : ℝ) := by
        intro x hx
        exact hc x hx
      -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to show that the integral is finite.
      have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => |Φ₂ r x|) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
        refine' HasFiniteIntegral.of_norm_bounded _ h₁ _
        -- Use the fact that |Φ₂ r x| ≤ c to show that the integral is finite.
        filter_upwards [self_mem_ae_restrict] with x hx using by
          have h₅ : x ∈ (Set.Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ) := hx
          have h₆ : |Φ₂ r x| ≤ (c : ℝ) := h₃ x h₅

            ‖(|Φ₂ r x| : ℝ)‖ = |Φ₂ r x| := by simp [norm_of_nonneg (abs_nonneg (Φ₂ r x))]
            _ ≤ (c : ℝ) := h₆
            _ = ‖(c : ℝ)‖ := by simp [norm_of_nonneg (by
              -- Show that c is non-negative
              have h₇ : ∀ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |Φ₂ r x| ≤ c := hc
              have h₈ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
                exact ⟨by linarith, by linarith⟩
              have h₉ : |Φ₂ r 0| ≤ c := h₇ 0 h₈
              have h₁₀ : 0 ≤ c := by
                have h₁₁ : 0 ≤ |Φ₂ r 0| := abs_nonneg _
                linarith
              exact h₁₀
            )]
      exact h₄
    -- Use the fact that the integral of |Φ₂ r x| is finite to show that the integral of Φ₂ r x is finite.
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
      have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the integral of |Φ₂ r x| is finite to show that the integral of Φ₂ r x is finite.
        refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_abs h₂
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  ) (by
    -- Use the fact that the measure of the complement of [0, 1] is 0.
    have h₁ : volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1) (Set.univ \ Set.Icc 0 1) = 0 := by
      simp [Set.ext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₁
  )⟩

we can directly use that `Φ₂` is continuous and the interval is compact. 

But Lean might need a direct proof using `HasFiniteIntegral`, perhaps by showing that the absolute value is bounded by an integrable function. 

Hmm, perhaps for the purpose of this problem, we can just assume that the fact that `Φ₂` is continuous on `[0,1]` is sufficient to automatically deduce that it has finite integral there, but perhaps Lean wants an explicit bound.

But let me try to prove it directly in the general case:

**Claim**: If `f : [a,b] → ℝ` is continuous, then `f` is integrable on `[a,b]`.

*Proof*: For any `ε > 0`, there exists `δ > 0` such that `|x - y| < δ ⇒ |f(x) - f(y)| < ε/(b - a)`. Then any partition with step `≤ δ` gives a Riemann sum differing by at most `ε`. So the Riemann integral exists, hence the Lebesgue integral does too. 

But perhaps Lean needs this to be formalized in terms of Bochner integration.

But perhaps we can try to use that `Φ₂ r` is continuous on `[0,1]`, and hence bounded, and `[0,1]` has finite volume, so `Φ₂ r` is finite a.e. and `∫ |Φ₂ r| < ∞`. 

Alternatively,

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₂ is continuous on [0,1] and has a finite integral there.
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      -- Apply the appropriate lemma to conclude the integral is finite.
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral_Icc
      <;> (try norm_num) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try norm_num at * <;> aesop)
    exact h₄
  exact h₁.integrableOn

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₂ r) x) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₂_r is continuous on a compact set and the integral is finite
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₂ r) x) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    -- Apply the lemma to establish the finite integral
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₂ r) x) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) :=
      h₃.hasFiniteVolume.hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuousOn h₂
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) := by
    continuity
  -- Use the fact that Φ₂ r is continuous on the compact set [0,1] to show it is integrable
  have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the set is compact to deduce finite integral
    have h₃ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (Set.Icc 0 1) := by
      exact Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₄ : IsCompact (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact sets are integrable
    have h₅ : (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) ∈ ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (Set.Icc 0 1) := h₃
    have h₆ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply isCompact_hasFiniteIntegral_Icc
      <;> (try simp_all [h₁, h₃, h₄])
      <;> (try continuity)
      <;> (try exact h₃)
    exact h₆
  exact h₂

have h : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₂ r is continuous on [0, 1] to show it is integrable
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    -- Apply the theorem that continuous functions on compact intervals are integrable
    have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the interval [0, 1] is compact and the function is continuous
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;> simp_all [measurableSet_Icc]
      <;> norm_num
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Show that the interval [0, 1] has finite volume
          apply isCompact_Icc.measurableSet
          <;> simp [hr]
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on [0, 1]
          exact h₁
        })
    exact h₂
  -- Use the fact that the function has a finite integral to conclude integrability
  simpa using h.integrableOn
exact h₂
exact ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral_Icc (by norm_num) Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn

have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 1)) := by sorry

have h : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- The function Φ₂(r, ε) is continuous on [0, 1] and hence bounded. Since [0, 1] is compact, the integral is finite.
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (fun ε : ℝ => Φ₂ r ε) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (fun ε : ℝ => (Φ₂ r ε : ℝ)) (Icc 0 1) := by
      exact h₁
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact sets are bounded and hence have finite integrals.
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun ε : ℝ => (Φ₂ r ε : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral_of_isCompact h₂ (by
        simpa [Set.Icc] using h₃)
    simpa using h₄
  exact h

have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₂ is continuous and the interval is bounded to show the integral is finite
    have h₃ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₄ : BddAbove (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
      use 1
      intro x hx
      simp only [Icc.mem_Icc] at hx ⊢
      linarith
    have h₅ : BddBelow (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
      use 0
      intro x hx
      simp only [Icc.mem_Icc] at hx ⊢
      linarith
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the interval is bounded to show the integral is finite
    have h₆ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the interval is bounded to show the integral is finite
      refine' ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral_restrict_Icc _ _ _
      · exact h₃
      · exact hr
      · norm_num
    exact h₆
  -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to show integrability
  simpa [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integrable] using h₂

-- Use the fact that Φ₂ is continuous on the closed interval [0, 1] and hence its absolute value is bounded there.
  have h₁ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => |(Φ₂ r) x|) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.abs
    exact Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  -- Find a bounded function that dominates |Φ₂ r| on [0, 1].
  have h₂ : ∃ (g : ℝ → ℝ), (∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, ENNReal.ofReal (g x)) < ⊤ ∧ ∀ x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |(Φ₂ r) x| ≤ g x := by
    -- Since Φ₂ is continuous on [0, 1], it is bounded by some M.
    have h₃ : ∃ M : ℝ, ∀ x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |(Φ₂ r) x| ≤ M := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₂ is continuous on a compact set to get boundedness.
      obtain ⟨M, hM⟩ := Metric.isCompact_Icc.exists_forall_ge (by continuity) (Set.nonempty_Icc.mpr (by norm_num))
      refine' ⟨M, _⟩
      intro x hx
      specialize hM x hx
      simp [abs_le] at hM ⊢
      <;>
      (try cases' le_total 0 ((Φ₂ r) x) with h h <;> simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos, le_of_lt]) <;>
      (try linarith) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
    -- Use the bound to construct a dominating function.
    obtain ⟨M, hM⟩ := h₃
    use fun x => M
    constructor
    · -- The integral of the constant function M over [0, 1] is M, which is finite.
      have h₄ : (∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, ENNReal.ofReal (M : ℝ)) = ENNReal.ofReal (M * 1) := by
        simp [MeasureTheory.integral_const, Icc_eq_empty]
        <;> field_simp [ENNReal.ofReal_mul]
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> simp_all [ENNReal.ofReal_one]
        <;> norm_num
      rw [h₄]
      <;> simp [ENNReal.ofReal_lt_top]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    · -- For all x in [0, 1], |Φ₂ r x| ≤ M.
      intro x hx
      specialize hM x hx
      linarith
  -- Apply the boundedness to conclude integrability.
  obtain ⟨g, hg₁, hg₂⟩ := h₂
  have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => |(Φ₂ r) x|) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    refine' (MeasureTheory.hasFiniteIntegral_of_dominated_by (fun x hx => hg₂ x hx) (by simpa using hg₁)).trans _
    -- The dominating function g has a finite integral, so |Φ₂ r| does as well.
    <;> simp_all [abs_nonneg]
  -- Since |Φ₂ r| has a finite integral, Φ₂ r is integrable.
  convert h₃.integrable_of_abs using 1 <;> simp [Real.norm_eq_abs]

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₄ : BddBelow (Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₂ r) x)) := by
      have h₅ : ∀ x, (Φ₂ r) x ≥ 0 := by
        intro x
        have h₆ : (Φ₂ r) x = (r * (x ^ 2 - 1)) ^ 2 := by
          simp [Φ₂]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₆]
        nlinarith [sq_nonneg (r * (x ^ 2 - 1))]
      refine' ⟨0, _⟩
      intro y
      rcases y with ⟨x, rfl⟩
      exact h₅ x
    have h₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;> aesop
    exact h₅
  exact h₁
exact (Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn.integrableOn_Icc).has_finite_integral

apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
  <;> simp_all [continuousOn_const, continuousOn_id, ContinuousOn.div]
  <;> apply ContinuousOn.mul
  <;> (try continuity) <;> (try apply ContinuousOn.mul) <;> (try continuity) <;>
    (try apply ContinuousOn.div) <;> (try continuity)
  <;> norm_num
  <;>
    (try
      {
        refine' ContinuousOn.mul (ContinuousOn.pow continuousOn_id 2) _
        continuity
      })
  <;>
    (try
      {
        refine' ContinuousOn.mul (ContinuousOn.pow continuousOn_id _) _
        continuity
      })
  <;>
    (try continuity)
  <;>
    (try norm_num)
  <;>
    (try
      {
        refine' ContinuousOn.div _ _
        · continuity
        · continuity
        · intro x hx
          have h₁ : 0 < x := by
            cases' hx with hx₁ hx₂
            linarith
          have h₂ : x ≤ 1 := by
            cases' hx with hx₁ hx₂
            linarith
          positivity
      })

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₂ is continuous on the interval [0, 1] and the interval is compact.
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) :=
      (hasFiniteIntegral_restrict_of_hasFiniteIntegral (by
        -- Prove that Φ₂ is integrable on [0, 1] using the fact that it is continuous on a compact interval.
        have h₄ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
        have h₅ : MeasurableOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := h₄.measurableOn
        have h₆ : HasFiniteIntegralOn (Φ₂ r) (measurableSet_Icc) := by
          -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact intervals are bounded and hence integrable.
          refine' ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegralOn _
          exact h₄
        -- Convert the hasFiniteIntegralOn statement to the required form.
        convert h₆ using 1
        <;> simp [MeasureTheory.volume]) : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) volume)
    exact h₃
  exact h₁.integrableOn

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₂ r x) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by sorry
  sorry

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ∃ (C : ℝ), ∀ (t : ℝ), t ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |(Φ₂ r) t| ≤ C := by
      use 1 + r ^ 2
      intro t ht
      simp only [Icc.mem_Icc] at ht
      have h₃ : 0 ≤ t := by linarith
      have h₄ : t ≤ 1 := by linarith
      simp [Φ₂, abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg, hr, h₃, le_of_lt]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try nlinarith [pow_two_nonneg (r - 1), pow_two_nonneg (r + 1)]) <;>
      (try
        {
          cases' le_or_lt 0 (r - t) with h₅ h₅ <;>
          cases' le_or_lt 0 (r + t) with h₆ h₆ <;>
          simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_neg, sub_nonneg, sub_nonpos] <;>
          nlinarith
        }) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
    -- Use the fact that the function is bounded and the interval is compact to conclude the integral is finite.
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      have h₄ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₅ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
      -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the interval is compact to bound the function.
      have h₆ : BddAbove (Set.range (fun t : ℝ => |(Φ₂ r) t|)) := by
        obtain ⟨C, hC⟩ := h₂
        use C
        intro x
        obtain ⟨t, rfl⟩ := x
        exact hC t (by
          simp_all [Icc.mem_Icc]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try nlinarith))
      -- Use the fact that the function is bounded and the interval is compact to conclude the integral is finite.
      have h₇ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
        have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₂ r) x) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
          -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the interval is compact to conclude the integral is finite.
          apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
          <;>
          (try assumption) <;>
          (try simp_all [Icc.mem_Icc]) <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try nlinarith)
        exact h₈
      exact h₇
    exact h₃
  -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
  have h₂ : ∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)), ‖(Φ₂ r) x‖ < ⊤ := by
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := h₁
    -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
    have h₄ : ∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)), ‖(Φ₂ r) x‖ < ⊤ := by
      -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
      refine' lt_top_iff_ne_top.mpr _
      intro h
      have h₅ : ¬HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
        intro h₆
        have h₇ : ∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)), ‖(Φ₂ r) x‖ ≠ ⊤ := by
          have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₂ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := h₆
          have h₉ : ∫⁻ (x : ℝ) in (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)), ‖(Φ₂ r) x‖ < ⊤ := by
            -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
            refine' lt_top_iff_ne_top.mpr _
            intro h₁₀
            -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
            simp_all [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integral_ne_top]
          -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
          linarith
        -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
        simp_all [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integral_ne_top]
      -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
      simp_all [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integral_ne_top]
    -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
    exact h₄
  -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof.
  simpa [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integral_ne_top] using h₁
successful_attempts:
have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₂ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) := Φ₂_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₂ : IntegrableOn (Φ₂ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) volume := by
    apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
    <;> (try norm_num) <;> (try linarith) <;> (try exact h₁)
  exact h₂.hasFiniteIntegral
